title: City of Mist Themebook
description: A questionnaire used during character creation to flesh out a single theme (Mythos, Logos, Crew, or Extra).

$schema.description: Optional pointer to this schema, used by IDE tooling.

type.description: Top-level category of theme this themebook produces.

name.description: Themebook name, e.g. ADAPTATION, DEFINING EVENT, ALLY.

categories.description: Optional comma/dot-separated list of category bullets that appear under the name (e.g. 'A SIDEKICK · A FAMILIAR · ...').

description.description: Prose description of what the theme covers.

concept.description: Concept section: optional concept questions and sentence starters that help clarify the theme idea before answering tag questions.

main_question.description: The headline concept question (typically marked with '?' in the book).

sentence_starters.description: Bullet sentence starters that prompt the player to flesh out the concept.

power_tag_questions.description: Lettered power tag questions. The first one is the core question and must be answered.

weakness_tag_questions.description: Lettered weakness tag questions. Each theme starts with one weakness tag.

extra_tags.description: Optional rules text about taking an extra power/weakness tag in lieu of doing it in another themebook.

kind.description: Mythos themes have a Mystery, Logos themes an Identity, Crew/Extra themes either.

context.description: Parenthetical concept hint shown alongside the example, e.g. 'Big heist'.

guidance_options.description: Bulleted suggestions for what kind of Mystery/Identity to choose.

crew_relationships.description: Optional list of relationship scenarios used after character creation when finalizing the crew.

theme_improvements.description: Named improvements that can be unlocked for this theme during play.

source.description: Bibliographic pointer to where in the source material this themebook is defined.

letter.description: The single-letter index of the question in the themebook (A-J typical).

question.description: The full question text. May be marked truncated when the source markdown lost text mid-line.

examples.description: Example tag answers shown in the book (italicized in the source).

truncated.description: True when the question text could not be fully recovered from the source markdown extraction.
